annotation.text:
This example stream demonstrates how the fictional energy provider Spark Energy cleans its raw electricity usage data before sending it for billing, removing duplicates and filtering out erroneous records.

Simulated usage events include intentional duplicates and malformed entries so you can see how the Deduplicate and Validate functions catch common data-quality issues.

Records rejected by Validate can be sent to Data Correction for repair and reprocessing.
Simulates a stream of electricity usage records, including intentional duplicates and malformed entries to exercise the cleaning logic.
Filter out duplicate events and records with missing or malformed fields. Rejected records go to Data Correction for repair and reprocessing.
Send the cleaned electricity usage records to the billing system.
